title: holcus lanatus subsp. tuberosus
intro: Holcus lanatus subsp. tuberosus, also known as the rough meadow-grass or tuberous oat-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Holcus lanatus subsp. tuberosus include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
